to people that already have car wash
buckets?
No matter what you sell, your prospect's

pitching car wash buckets on a cold
call, you're going to hear we have a
vendor for that or I'm not interested
all day long.
So, what are you supposed to do?
The way out is to shine a light on a
potential problem
with their car wash bucket without
having assumptions that they actually

Here's what that might sound like.
So, Josh, I'm not sure if this is
happening with you,
but a lot of times when people wash
their car,
dirt can settle to the bottom of the

and get trapped in the sponge and can

create these little swirl marks.
How are you making sure that doesn't

One of two things will happen. They'll
either say, "I don't wash my car. I
don't care or someone else washes it."
And that's great because it's not a fit
for what you do. Or they'll say, "That's
a good question. Like, I kind of just
rinse the sponge off and kind of try to

this.
You've probably looked into
a grit guard.
And they might say, "Well, what's that?"
And you say, "With a grit guard, you rub

dirt settles to the bottom of the bucket
and off your sponge so you don't scratch

and listen. Or they say, "I have heard
of a grit guard."
And then you might take a guess as to

Sounds like you used it, but it didn't

shut the front door and listen. Here's
the thing, it's not your job to fill
people's heads with information when you
cold call. Your job is to draw it out.
Buyers have the answers. Sellers have
the questions.

transcript.text: Here's something that helped me remain a
little more relaxed and chill. When I
cold called, I would remind myself of
Major League Baseball players. They
don't get on base most of the time. In
fact, they strike out most of the time.
I think even the best of the world only
get on base about 36% of the time. And
if you watch them play, it doesn't seem
to phase them too much. Like, if they
strike out, they're like, "Whatever."
And they go to the dugout. And if they
get on base, they're like, "Okay,
whatever." And they get on first base.
That's because they know striking out
and getting on base are part of the same
experience. It's the same thing with
cold calling. Whenever I was focused on
getting on base, I would be disappointed
when I didn't, especially if I was
making two or three calls and they
didn't go well. But what I discovered
was if I made 46 calls, I would always
have at least two or three positive
conversations would last two or three
minutes and some would even book a
meeting. And knowing that just made me
relax a lot more because I would know
that if I just made the dials, things
would always work out for me. The muscle
to work is to step up to the plate,
swing 46 times or 47 times, and know
that things will always work out for you
if you just keep on swinging. Detach
from the outcome.

transcript.text: Prospect says, "I need some time to
think about it. What do you say?" There
are two ways to respond. Way number one
is based on traditional sales or sales
1.0. It's aimed at handling it,
overcoming it, pressuring the prospect.
It sounds like this. You don't need more
time to think about it. What you need is
more information. And since I'm the only
one that has information, what
information do you need? Or maybe you
try to create some false scarcity. Well,
we can include these features if you
sign up in the next couple weeks. or the
price is only good until the end of the
month. Each of these responses sends a
clear message to the prospect that the
salesperson is putting their best
interest first so they can smell your
commission breath. People retreat when
they're being forced into a decision.
That's because autonomy is a basic human
need. But there's another way you can
respond, and that is simply to let go of
trying to move the sale forward, and
instead try to lean back and understand
the truth. Here's what that might sound
like. Prospect says, "I need some time
to think about it." You lean back,
detach, and you say in a calm voice,
"I'm guessing you thought the price was
going to be much less." And then you
shut the front door and listen. This is
called elicitation. And one of two
things will happen. The prospect will
correct you or they'll confirm. Either
way, you've unlocked more truth. And in
sales, knowing how to unlock truth is a
good skill to learn and master because
without truth, there's no trust. And
without trust, there's no transaction.

transcript.text: Imagine the scenario. You walk into a
department store and within seconds a
salesperson approaches you and with
unbridled enthusiasm says this, "Hey,
welcome to ABC. My name is Josh. How can
I help you?" If you're like most people,
you're going to retreat because that
energy feels off-putting. It's like
you're going to be told what to do or
pressured into buying something. As
sellers, we create this by what we say
and what our intent is. On a cold call,
hey, my name is Josh. the reason for my
call is creates the same reaction. So
what's the way out of this? It starts by
changing our intent. When our intent is
to sell everyone and assume that
everyone needs what we're buying, we
sound pushy because intent affects what
we say. But if we change our intent from
how can I sell to is there a problem
here? It changes everything because
we're no longer assuming what we have is
needed. We're shifting from convincing
and explaining to asking a neutral
question about a potential problem
without leading people to a desired
answer. So, here's what that might sound
like on a cold call. Prospect picks up.
Let's say it's me.
In a calm voice, the seller says this.
Hey, Josh. My name is Nick. we've never
spoken before, but I was on your
LinkedIn, been following you for a
while, and was wondering if I could ask
you a question. I would say, "Sure, how
could I help you, Nick?" And then Nick
says this, "So Josh, over the course of
3 months, your posts have had a little
over 4,100 comments.
How are you identifying the commenters
that might be a fit for your workshop so
you can reach out to them proactively?
Is that something you're doing manually,
or do you have a way to surface them
automatically?
and shut the front door and listen.
You're not assuming, you're exploring.
You're asking a question that makes
people scratch their head and think,
"hm, I'm not sure. That's a good
question. What do you mean?" And now
we're in a conversation.
You will start way more conversations
when you come from a place of, "Is there
a problem?" and assuming there is one.
Buyers have the answers.

videoDetails.title: How to Sell Without Selling | The Sidekick Life with Franchise Sidekick & Becc Holland
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Sales has a PR problem—and Becc Holland is here to fix it.

In this episode of The Sidekick Life, Becc Holland, CEO of Flip the Script, talks about how to sell without selling: no pressure, no persuasion tactics, no “overcoming objections.” Just a buyer-first method built on trust, clarity, and diagnosis.

Becc has been working closely with the Franchise Sidekick team leading into one of their biggest conferences, and her approach is completely different than the “one script fits all” sales training you usually see. Instead of teaching people how to talk better, she teaches teams how to learn the buyer better than the buyer knows themselves—and how to guide people away from bad decisions, not toward quick closes.

We also dive into Becc’s honest first impressions of franchising (yes… Wolf of Wall Street vibes 😅), what surprised her most about franchise buyers, and why the stakes feel even higher when someone is making a life-changing financial decision for their family.

In this episode, we cover:
✅ Why most sales training is built around manipulation (even when it uses “soft” language)
✅ The real reason buyers don’t trust sellers—and how to reverse it
✅ Why “rapport” isn’t the goal (and why trust is)
✅ The best talk/listen ratio for intro calls (10–15% talking)
✅ Why referrals convert so well (and what that teaches sellers)
✅ How misaligned expectations create failure—especially in major purchases like business ownership
✅ How to build a buyer resource guide so new sales hires actually understand the customer

Interested in franchising or business ownership?
We break down our process in a free 20-minute webinar:
🎥 10,000 Locations & the Franchise Buyer’s Blueprint: https://webinars.franchisesidekick.com
videoDetails.author: Flip the Script

videoDetails.title: The 2026 Cold (Outreach) Olympics: The Always Be Closing Ceremonies
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Welcome to Day 17 of The Cold (Outreach) Olympics 2026 by Flip the Script!

On February 23 @ 3pm CET (9am ET), we’ll be broadcasting LIVE from Milan to present:

THE ALWAYS BE CLOSING CEREMONIES: CLOSING IT OUT– & HOW TO LAND “THE JUMP” (TO YOUR NEXT ROLE 🙂)

Here’s what we’ll be covering:

↳ How to Make the Jump from SDR to AE
↳ How to Make the Jump from SMB AE to Enterprise (or Become a Top Performer)
↳ How to Make the Jump from AE to Leader
↳ The Things that Your Manager Will NEVER Tell You (That Matter)
↳ Closing Crash-Course Review: The 8 SDR Sessions
↳ Closing Crash-Course Review: The 8 AE Sessions
↳ The #1 Most IMPORTANT Thing You Can Do to Be Successful– Regardless of your role.

______________________________

▶️  Learn how to prospect, close deals, and build pipeline through Outreach here:

www.outreach.io

▶️ Subscribe to our Youtube Channel for more sales content: 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEW00AJeLgV8MtvLcDxLn7w?sub_confirmation=1

▶️ Follow us on Linkedin for up-to-date insights on the sales industry: 

https://www.linkedin.com/company/flip-the-script

▶️ Visit our site to gain access to all of our written & video content: 

https://www.flipthescript.com/

videoDetails.title: The 2026 Cold (Outreach) Olympics: Day 16
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Welcome to Day 16 of The Cold (Outreach) Olympics 2026 by Flip the Script!

On February 21 @ 3pm CET (9am ET), we’ll be broadcasting LIVE from Milan to present:

THE “HOW TO” OF DEAL REVIEWS, + FORECASTING, + DEAL STAGE CRITERIA– & HOW TO PREVENT DEAL SLIPPAGE, BEFORE IT’S TOO LATE

Here’s what we’ll be covering:

↳ What Criteria to Objectively Measure– In a Deal Review

videoDetails.title: How to Turn Cold Emails into High-Impact Conversations | The Emblazers Show ft. Becc Holland
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Are your sales reps struggling to break through to buyers who won't respond?
In this episode of The Emblazers Show, host Tim Riesterer sits down with Becc Holland, CEO and Founder of Flip the Script, to rethink what modern prospecting actually looks like.

Becc shares why high-volume, spray-and-pray tactics are dead, and what to do instead. From uncovering problems buyers didn't even know they had, to crafting cold outreach that actually gets replies, this conversation is packed with practical strategies sales leaders and reps can put to work immediately.
In this episode, you'll learn:

- Why traditional prospecting fails and what's replaced it
- How to use buyer psychology and metrics to create new demand
- The step-by-step method for uncovering "unknown problems" that spark real conversations
- Cold email best practices: deliverability, readability, and subject lines that work
- How sales leaders can equip their teams to become trusted industry advisors

Episode highlights:
(00:00) Introduction
(00:52) Meet Becc Holland, CEO of Flip the Script
(02:08) How prospecting has changed from old tactics to today’s reality
(04:37) Current email response rates and why old volume-based methods fail
(06:18) Uncovering problems buyers didn’t know they had
(10:35) How to spark self-persuasion and confidence in buyers
(11:15) Step-by-step method for uncovering buyer metrics and gremlins
(22:00) Leadership perspective on routing, sequences, and personalization
(24:25) Email optimization
(33:25) Equip reps to become industry experts and tactical leaders
(38:22) Leading from the front and enabling success

______________________________

▶️ Subscribe to our Youtube Channel for more sales content: 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEW00AJeLgV8MtvLcDxLn7w?sub_confirmation=1

▶️ Follow us on Linkedin for up-to-date insights on the sales industry: 

https://www.linkedin.com/company/flip-the-script

▶️ Visit our site to gain access to all of our written & video content: 

https://www.flipthescript.com/

videoDetails.title: The 2026 Cold (Outreach) Olympics: Day 14
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Welcome to Day 14 of The Cold (Outreach) Olympics 2026 by Flip the Script!

On February 19 @ 3pm CET (9am ET), we’ll be broadcasting LIVE from Milan to present:

WHAT TO COACH AEs ON TO 4X CONVERSION

Here’s what we’ll be covering:

↳ What you should look for (specifically): On DISCO Calls
↳ Top Tips of Effective Coaching
↳ How to Structure a Coaching Session
↳ How to Measure Progress: & Drive Improvement Over Time
↳ Coaching Logisitics: When to listen to calls, how many, how much, & what to Listen For
↳ How to Self-Coach as an AE– Or Encourage Reps to Self-Coach

______________________________

▶️  Learn how to prospect, close deals, and build pipeline through Outreach here:

www.outreach.io

▶️ Subscribe to our Youtube Channel for more sales content: 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEW00AJeLgV8MtvLcDxLn7w?sub_confirmation=1

▶️ Follow us on Linkedin for up-to-date insights on the sales industry: 

https://www.linkedin.com/company/flip-the-script

▶️ Visit our site to gain access to all of our written & video content: 

https://www.flipthescript.com/

videoDetails.title: The 2026 Cold (Outreach) Olympics: Day 15
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Welcome to Day 15 of The Cold (Outreach) Olympics 2026 by Flip the Script!

On February 20 @ 3pm CET (9am ET), we’ll be broadcasting LIVE from Milan to present:

HOW TO COACH, TRAIN, & BUILD A LETHAL SDR TEAM— THAT EXCEEDS QUOTA LONG TERM

Here’s what we’ll be covering:

↳ The Biggest Pitfalls For SDRs ( & what to watch out for)
↳ The 3 Ds that Damage SDRs the Most: Data, Decay, & Deliverability
↳ How to Diagnose Where Your SDRs are Struggling
↳ Coaching: The Top Things to Coach On
↳ Coaching: The Do’s & Don’ts of Building a Coaching Cadence & Rubrik
↳ How to Build a Stellar SDR Team— That Consistently Outperforms Their Quota

______________________________

▶️  Learn how to prospect, close deals, and build pipeline through Outreach here:

www.outreach.io

▶️ Subscribe to our Youtube Channel for more sales content: 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEW00AJeLgV8MtvLcDxLn7w?sub_confirmation=1

▶️ Follow us on Linkedin for up-to-date insights on the sales industry: 

https://www.linkedin.com/company/flip-the-script

▶️ Visit our site to gain access to all of our written & video content: 

https://www.flipthescript.com/

videoDetails.title: The 2026 Cold (Outreach) Olympics: Day 13
videoDetails.shortDescription:
Welcome to Day 13 of The Cold (Outreach) Olympics 2026 by Flip the Script!

On February 18 @ 3pm CET (9am ET), we’ll be broadcasting LIVE from Milan to present:

🥶 COLDDDDD EMAIL… & HOW TO WRITE ONE THAT JUST WORKS

Here’s what we’ll be covering:

↳ How to Land Emails, in the Inbox (Email Deliverability)— Words to say & not say.
↳ Email Readability & Subject lines
↳ The 5 Types of Emails (EX. break-up v. bump)-- & What Content to Include
↳ Email Relevance + Personalization
↳ The Best Questions to Ask in Emails
↳ Cold Email Math— Activity metrics, results, how many to send, & when to stop.

______________________________

▶️  Learn how to prospect, close deals, and build pipeline through Outreach here:

www.outreach.io

▶️ Subscribe to our Youtube Channel for more sales content: 

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEW00AJeLgV8MtvLcDxLn7w?sub_confirmation=1

▶️ Follow us on Linkedin for up-to-date insights on the sales industry: 

https://www.linkedin.com/company/flip-the-script

▶️ Visit our site to gain access to all of our written & video content: 

https://www.flipthescript.com/

videoDetails.title: Using LinkedIn for warm outreach by Thibaut Souyris
videoDetails.shortDescription:
This is a recording of my keynote for the 2021 Startup Sales Summit. I talked about the T-shaped Sales Development Framework and how you can use it to do warm outreach on LinkedIn.

You can download the worksheet here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_ix_CE_rqafSBnvbwWI7CTz2uxyKsLS3/view?usp=sharing

You can download the slides here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1RMb9UJBKHfJCHW7xS63XWA2uFhnJwCD1/view?usp=sharing

And if you want more free resources, check this page: https://www.saleslabs.io/linkedin-welcome-page/
videoDetails.author: SalesLabs
